note: Drop a block BESIDE a pane inside a layout — drag a block over a composed layout and it now targets the pane under your finger and adds the new block on the side you're nearest (left/right/top/bottom). It flattens into the row when the side runs along it, and wraps the pane perpendicular otherwise — so you can finally add to the RIGHT of a pane that sits in a vertical stack (e.g. right of the chart), which wasn't possible before (no pre-existing seam there). Replaces the old between-seams-only insert with one unified 'add beside the pane you point at' rule.

Fixed the wiggle/detach drag-border SIZING — the green pane faces were computed from the tree's defaultSize, so once you RESIZED a card (which renders it through the responsive renderer, reflowing/stacking the panes) the borders drifted off the real panes. They now MEASURE the actual rendered panes, so the faces sit exactly on them whether or not the card is resized / stacked / nested. (Verified headless: resized narrow → panes stacked → faces aligned with 0px offset.)

Deploy fix — the staging build broke because crouton-devtools loads @fyit/crouton-feedback at build time (from #976). Two parts: (1) declare the dep on the POC so pnpm's strict node_modules can resolve it, and (2) add it to the deploy's layerPackages so CI builds its dist (it wasn't in the build set, so the module file never existed). User-invisible; unblocks the preview deploy.

Per-block DISPLAY VARIANTS — a block can now offer bounded layouts of the same data (the Artists list: Rows · Cards · Table). Select a pane in the edit view → a Display chip row lets you (or an agent) flip the variant; the choice is a bounded enum declared on the registry and serialised on the leaf, so it persists and round-trips. This is the 'collection viewer options' ask, kept inside the expressiveness boundary (an enum, not free config).

Block sizing as DECLARED DATA — each block in the registry now carries a sizing descriptor ({ width, height: 'fill' | 'hug' }), so 'the component decides its own size' is one source the renderer and an agent both read, not per-component CSS. A Top bar / Bottom nav declare height:'hug', so a pinned bar comes out SHORT because the BLOCK says so (not because of where it's pinned) — no per-instance size control. Honoured in the page Preview (a hug block → a short bar, a fill block grows).

Cleanup pass — ripped out the dormant survey/viewport code left over after the slider→per-element-resize pivot (and the dead fitOverview), so the board path is one clear model. Chart block bars now render reliably (each bar sits in a definite-height track, so its % height resolves). And the board hides Vue Flow's own ⛶ fit button — it zooms into our oversized cards; the pill Fit is the right one (the Site flow keeps its ⛶).

Fixed the selection highlight not scrolling with the content in the edit view — a tall layout scrolls inside the frame, so the dim/focus overlay now re-measures on scroll and stays locked to the selected pane.

Removed the global responsive slider — responsiveness is now PER-ELEMENT (drag a card's resize handle to preview its layout at that width). Opening edit now lands at the node's RESIZED width. And blocks decide their own size intrinsically (the Top bar / Bottom nav are short bars), so a pinned Top bar comes out as a proper short pill with content scrolling under it — no per-instance flex controls.
Bigger resize handle — the per-node corner resize grip is now a clearly grabbable button with a diagonal-resize icon (was a tiny corner mark, hard to hit on mobile).

Distinct demo blocks — the palette now has visibly different blocks (Artists List with avatars · New form · Stats · Bookings Chart · a Top bar · a Bottom nav · Spacer) instead of every card rendering the same KPI tile. Now you can SEE which block landed where — and the Top bar / Bottom nav are the natural things to pin top/bottom.

Per-node RESIZE handle — select a card and drag its bottom-right corner to set its width (and height); the card then previews its layout RESPONSIVELY at that width (panes reflow), so each element is its own width preview. Double-click the handle to reset. Also: you can now DELETE a block/layout from the canvas (trash button in the node toolbar). [Step 1 of replacing the global slider with per-element resize.]

Fixed the weird half-pane selection highlight in the edit view when a block is tucked into a gutter tab — the dim/focus overlay now only draws when it can measure the real visible panes (a collapsed/tabbed block just has no on-canvas dim instead of a misplaced box).

Page REGIONS — pin a node to the top/bottom edge (a sticky pill) from its toolbar, then ▶ Preview assembles the running page (pinned pills + scrolling main): the review-flow + pill-top + pill-bottom shape. Also: wiggle/drag faces now line up with the rendered panes (were footprint-based, drifted on uneven splits); survey is read-only (no accidental pane resize); and opening the edit view lands at the width you were surveying at.

New Spacer block — add empty space from the palette and snap/resize it like any block to push others around or hold a gap (renders a faint dashed hint in the builder). Also fixed POC block components not resolving in the renderer (they're now registered globally), which also makes the drop-ghost preview actually show.

Fixed selecting a block in the edit view at narrow widths — the panes reflow (a horizontal split stacks vertically when narrow) but selection was hit-testing the un-reflowed layout, so you'd select the wrong/sliver pane; it now hit-tests the real rendered panes

Zoom-to-fit (bottom-left Fit) frames the board by the real card geometry now (it was using Vue Flow's measurement, which is smaller than the cards, so it zoomed into a corner); pinch-to-zoom works while the responsive slider is active (was disabled outside edit); live reorder preview — panes shift to show where a dragged block lands
Single-item layouts are snappable now — hover a dragged block OVER a single block (not just a multi-pane layout) and it snaps beside it into a split (edge picked from which half you're over)

Real fixes (found by reproducing in a headless browser): pull-to-reorder WORKS now — it was measuring against Vue Flow's node wrapper, which is smaller than the visible card, so every in-card drag read as 'outside' → always detach; pinch-to-zoom over a layout works (it read the wrong transform element); opening edit flies from the card at the flow's CURRENT zoom; Site-flow cards are icon-only (cleaner)
Attempted reorder fix via a detach margin (didn't work — the real bug was the wrong measurement element, fixed in v33)
Site-flow page cards now show their settings condensed — live/draft status (coloured dot) · permissions/visibility · layout · in-nav — and tapping a card opens the full page
Fluid reorder — while pulling a pane, drag it ACROSS to another slot to reorder (green 'move here'), or OUT of the card to detach; the panes FLIP into their new order
Nested drop targets the NEAREST seam across all splits — parent reachable near top-level gaps, child near its own seams (no longer always the child)
Always-on responsive slider drives ALL layouts (no Responsive button; max = edit, scrub = preview); actions moved to a top icon pill; Fit sits left of the slider
Drop INTO a nested layout — insert recurses to the deepest split under your finger (path-addressed), not just the top-level split
Responsive mode gets a continuous width slider (scrub device width live, like the breakpoint author); scroll an overflowing preview instead of panning the canvas; prettier changelog timeline
Consolidated header (page identity + back button); version chip opens this changelog; zoom controls moved to top-right
Undo button + ⌘Z (board history)
Page header mirrors the real crouton-pages model (status · visibility · settings · preview)
Page opens into a framed container with an identity header (icon · name · path · access · status)
Smooth page⇄flow cross-fade (simultaneous, no bg flash, fit preserved)
Pinch-zoom over a layout; added block lands clear, centered, with a green active glow
Ghost slot mirrors the dragged item's shape & size
Insert-snap arms reliably over a layout (overlap detection + steady dwell)
Panes ease apart to open a real slot for the dragged item
Ghost slab at the armed insert point (this is where it lands)
Dropped the "release to snap" tag (green ring is enough)
Light-green glow on the card you are dragging
Panes FLIP into place after a detach/insert (renderer, #943)
Page-open zoom transition (later reverted — broke fit, flashed bg)
Earlier spikes (1–11): board · drag/snap/merge · pull-to-detach · ✨ Magic/compile · focus edit view · Site⇄Page flow · page promotion · instant centering
